class OrderService:
    def create_order(self, user_id, cart_items):
        # 1. Calculate Total
        total = sum(item.price for item in cart_items)
        
        # 2. Check Stock (Complex logic)
        for item in cart_items:
            if item.stock < 1: raise ValueError("Out of stock")
            
        # 3. Save to DB
        order = self.repo.save_order(user_id, total, cart_items)
        
        # 4. Trigger Email (Async)
        send_email_task.delay(user_id, "Order Confirmed")
        return order

lessons.title: 15.3 Project 2: Real-Time Chat App (WebSockets)
lessons.content:
1. Project Scope

  • **Core:** FastAPI WebSockets, Redis (Pub/Sub).
  • **Advanced:** Online Status, Typing Indicators, Chat History.
  • **Frontend:** Simple HTML/JS client.

2. The "Real-Time" Challenge

HTTP/REST is "Ask -> Answer". WebSockets are "Always Connected". We use Redis to sync messages across multiple server workers.

3. Code Snippet (Socket Manager)

sockets.py
class ConnectionManager:
    def __init__(self):
        self.active_connections: list[WebSocket] = []

    async def connect(self, websocket: WebSocket):
        await websocket.accept()
        self.active_connections.append(websocket)

    async def broadcast(self, message: str):
        for connection in self.active_connections:
            await connection.send_text(message)

manager = ConnectionManager()

lessons.title: 15.4 Project 3: AI PDF Summarizer (SaaS Wrapper)
lessons.content:
1. Project Scope

  • **Core:** Upload PDF -> Extract Text -> Send to GPT-4 -> Return Summary.
  • **Advanced:** Rate Limiting (Free vs Pro users), Stripe Integration (Mock).
  • **Tech:** LangChain or OpenAI API directly.

2. Business Logic

This is a real-world SaaS pattern. Users pay for " Intelligence". You manage the API costs and credits.

3. Code Snippet (Logic)

ai_worker.py
async def summarize_pdf(file_path):
    # 1. Extract Text
    text = extract_text_from_pdf(file_path)
    
    # 2. Chunk if too long (Token limits)
    chunks = split_text(text, 2000)
    
    # 3. Summarize Map-Reduce
    summaries = []
    for chunk in chunks:
        res = await openai.ChatCompletion.create(..., content=f"Summarize: {chunk}")
        summaries.append(res)
        
    return " ".join(summaries)

lessons.title: 15.5 Documentation: The README.md
lessons.content:
1. Learning Outcomes

  • Why code without docs is trash.
  • Structure: Title, Demo GIF, Tech Stack, "How to Run".
  • Adding Badges (CI Passing, License).

2. The "30 Second Rule"

If I can't figure out how to run your app in 30 seconds reading your README, I'm closing the tab.

lessons.title: 15.6 Hosting: Deploying Your Portfolio
lessons.content:
1. Learning Outcomes

  • Buying a domain (optional but recommended).
  • Using Vercel (Frontend) + Render/Railway (Backend).
  • Setting up HTTPS (SSL) automatically.

2. Implementation

Connect your GitHub repo to Render. Set build command: `pip install -r requirements.txt`. Set Start command: `gunicorn main:app`. Done.

lessons.title: 15.7 Creating Demo Videos (Loom/OBS)
lessons.content:
1. Learning Outcomes

  • Why Video > Live Demo (Live demos break).
  • Scripting a 60-second walkthrough.
  • Embedding video in LinkedIn/Resume.

2. Script Template

"Hi, I'm [Name]. This is [App]. It solves [Problem]. Here is me logging in... here is the feature... and here is the result. Thanks."

lessons.title: 15.8 Final Polish & Code Review
lessons.content:
1. Checklist

  • Remove `print()` statements and hardcoded secrets.
  • Run `ruff` one last time.
  • Ensure `.gitignore` excludes `__pycache__` and `.env`.
  • Add license (MIT).

2. The "Grand Release"

Push your final commits. Pin the repositories on your GitHub profile. You are now ready to apply.

lessons.title: 16.1 The 3 Paths: Backend vs Data vs DevOps
lessons.content:
1. Learning Outcomes

  • **Backend:** Building APIs, Architectures. (Django, FastAPI).
  • **Data/AI:** Analyzing numbers, training models. (Pandas, PyTorch).
  • **DevOps:** Automating infrastructure. (Docker, Terraform, Ansible).

2. The "Day in Life" Comparison

Backend: "Why is this API slow?"
Data: "Why is this chart weird?"
DevOps: "Why is the server down?"

lessons.title: 16.2 Contributing to Open Source (Your First PR)
lessons.content:
1. Learning Outcomes

  • Finding "Good First Issues".
  • Forking, Cloning, Branching, Pushing.
  • Writing a good Pull Request description.

2. The Imposter Syndrome

You don't need to rewrite the Linux Kernel. Fixing a typo in documentation IS a valid contribution.

lessons.title: 16.3 Freelancing & Indie Hacking with Python
lessons.content:
1. Learning Outcomes

  • Platforms: Upwork vs Fiverr vs Toptal.
  • Niches: "Web Scraping Automation" is HUGE for Python freelancers.
  • Pricing: Fixed Price vs Hourly (Start Fixed to build rep).

2. The Automation Niche

Clients pay good money for: "Take this Excel sheet, scrape these sites, and email me the result daily." Python is the KING of this.

lessons.title: 16.4 Corporate Survival Guide: The First 90 Days
lessons.content:
1. Learning Outcomes

  • Setup Phase: Get access to Jira, Git, AWS ASAP.
  • The "Bus Factor": Document everything you learn.
  • Asking Questions: "5 minutes of research saves 5 minutes of shame".

2. The Golden Rule

Don't be the person who breaks Production on Friday at 5 PM. If you do, own it immediately.

lessons.title: 16.5 Python in 2030: Mojo, Rust Integration, and AI
lessons.content:
1. Learning Outcomes

  • **Mojo:** A new language that looks like Python but runs as fast as C.
  • **Rust + Python:** Using `PyO3` to write high-performance extensions.
  • **AI Coding:** Why you will be an "AI Orchestrator", not just a "Coder".

2. Don't Panic

Python is not dying. It is becoming the "Interface Layer" for high-performance engines written in Rust/C/Mojo.

lessons.title: 16.6 Certifications: Worth it or Scam?
lessons.content:
1. Learning Outcomes

  • **Cloud Certs (AWS/Azure):** Highly Valuable.
  • **Language Certs (PCEP/PCAP):** Mostly ignore. Projects > standard Python certs.
  • **Specialized (TensorFlow/CKAD):** Good for mid-level niches.

lessons.title: 16.7 Networking for Introverts
lessons.content:
1. Learning Outcomes

  • Twitter/X Tech Twitter: Actually useful for finding jobs.
  • Meetups: Go to listen, not to talk (initially).
  • The "Build in Public" movement.

2. Action Item

Post your Capstone Project on LinkedIn/Twitter with a video demo. Tag the libraries you used (e.g., @FastAPI).

lessons.title: 16.8 Final Assessment & Next Steps
lessons.content:
The End? No, The Beginning.

You have completed the entire Python Engineering Path. You know more than 90% of applicants.

One Last Task

Create a **"Career Notion Page"** tracking:

  • Jobs Applied (Company, Status, Notes).
  • Skills to Learn (Next).
  • Network Contacts.

Certificate of Completion

🎉 You are a Python Engineer. Go build the future. 🎉
